themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to housing, social services, and public aid. This theme appears prominently in Spain, France, Italy, and Norway, with parties like Vox, Rassemblement National, and FrP advocating for these measures as a core electoral strategy.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Accuses regional assemblies of censoring questions about 'national priority' in housing, healthcare, and education; demands prioritization of Spaniards in Andalusia and other regions.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Proposes taxing TotalEnergies' excess profits to aid French households if fuel price caps fail; advocates for economic policies favoring French citizens.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Criticizes opposition for blocking policies that prioritize Italians in social housing and public services; Salvini announces rent support for separated parents.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Demands stricter immigration controls and prioritization of Norwegian citizens in welfare policies, citing cultural and economic concerns.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Campaigns on transparency and housing policies, emphasizing national identity and opposition to mass immigration.

themes.title: Trump’s Iran war dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right media in the U.S., UK, France, Italy, and Spain are heavily focused on Trump's handling of the Iran war, including military actions, diplomatic negotiations, and economic impacts like rising fuel prices. The coverage often frames Trump's approach as decisive and necessary, despite criticism from opposition parties and international allies.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's administration reviews Iran's peace proposals, rejects terms, and faces criticism for troop withdrawals from Germany and rising fuel prices.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Criticizes U.S. troop withdrawals from Germany and supports Trump's stance on Iran, framing it as a necessary response to global threats.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella criticize U.S. policies but align with Trump's tough stance on Iran, emphasizing national security and economic impacts.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government aligns with Trump's Iran policies, emphasizing Italy's role in transatlantic security and criticizing opposition for undermining U.S.-Italy relations.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Supports Trump's hardline stance on Iran, criticizing EU and Spanish government for perceived weakness in handling the conflict.

themes.title: Meloni’s government longevity sparks cross-national debate
themes.blurb: Giorgia Meloni's government in Italy has become the second-longest in the country's post-war history, sparking debates about stability, governance, and policy effectiveness. This theme resonates in Germany, Hungary, France, and beyond, where far-right parties are using Meloni's tenure as a model for their own political strategies.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government reaches 1,288 days in power, becoming Italy's second-longest; faces criticism over economic stagnation and unfulfilled promises.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD cites Meloni's longevity as evidence of far-right governance viability, pushing for similar stability in Germany.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungarian media contrasts Meloni's stability with Orbán's recent electoral defeat, sparking debates about governance models.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella reference Meloni's success as a blueprint for far-right governance in France, emphasizing stability and national sovereignty.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Far-right parties in Germany, France, Sweden, Norway, and the UK are experiencing significant surges in polls and local elections. This theme highlights the growing influence of parties like AfD, Rassemblement National, Sverigedemokraterna, and Reform UK, which are reshaping political landscapes with nationalist and anti-immigration agendas.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads polls in Thuringia and Saxony-Anhalt, gains ground in national surveys, and faces internal power struggles.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Le Pen and Bardella lead polls for the 2027 presidential election, despite Le Pen's past legal challenges.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD gains influence in Sweden, pushing for stricter immigration policies and challenging traditional parties in polls.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP leads national polls in Norway, with Listhaug positioning herself as a potential prime minister.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage's party gains traction in UK local elections, challenging Conservatives in traditional strongholds.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric in the U.S., UK, Italy, and Sweden is increasingly linked to political violence, including protests, threats, and physical attacks. This theme highlights how inflammatory language from leaders and media outlets is contributing to a rise in extremism and unrest.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's rhetoric linked to multiple assassination attempts and violent protests; supporters clash with opponents.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage's inflammatory language sparks protests and alleged attacks on Reform UK candidates during campaign events.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government faces backlash over perceived authoritarian rhetoric, including protests and comparisons to fascist regimes.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD's anti-immigration rhetoric linked to rising tensions and violent incidents, including attacks on protesters.
